verdict_reasoning: IZUMO4 is a member of the IZUMO family of proteins. Unlike IZUMO1, which is a sperm-surface protein critical for fertilization, IZUMO4 (also known as CRISP7 or related to the IZUMO family) appears to be primarily an intracellular/secreted protein. Based on available knowledge, IZUMO4 lacks a canonical transmembrane domain and does not have well-documented stable surface expression on somatic cells. It is not known to be GPI-anchored or stably co-trafficked as a surface complex partner. Given lack of strong evidence for outer leaflet exposure, verdict is no.
